text:
Once upon a time in the lush, verdant forest of Evergreen, there lived a young qilin named Lumi. Lumi was special not just because she was a qilin, but also because she had the rare ability to communicate with plants and animals. Her fur shimmered like the dew on the morning grass, and her eyes sparkled like the stars above.

One day, as Lumi wandered through the forest, she noticed a group of saplings struggling to grow due to a nearby river that had dried up during a long, scorching summer. The leaves of the trees were withering, and the small animals that usually played among them had become quiet and sad.

Lumi knew she had to help. She remembered a powerful spell her grandmother had taught her, one that could bring water from even the driest earth. She approached the oldest tree, a mighty oak with roots that seemed to stretch to the very heart of the forest.

"Lady Oak," Lumi
